description: Documented malicious or compromised NuGet packages. NuGet supply-chain incidents 2022-2024 cover SeroXen RAT delivery campaigns, cracked-commercial-component typosquats, and dependency-confusion attempts against Azure DevOps internal feeds.

entries.name: Guna.UI2.WinForms-typosquat
entries.description: Typosquat of the commercial Guna UI library. Shipped a cracked DLL bundled with the SeroXen RAT; downloaded thousands of times before takedown.

entries.name: Moq
entries.description: Maintainer added a closed-source SponsorLink DLL that scanned local git config for developer emails and hashed them client-side. Triggered widespread distrust and ecosystem backlash; was removed in 4.20.2.

entries.name: DiscordRichPresence-typosquat
entries.description: Cluster of typosquats targeting .NET Discord developers. Payloads delivered token grabbers and remote-access trojans tailored for Windows desktop builds.

entries.name: Coinbase.Core-typosquat
entries.description: Typosquat of an unofficial Coinbase SDK with a wallet-key stealer payload. Triggered on first import via static constructor.

entries.name: SeroXen-RAT-campaign
entries.description: Phylum 2023 disclosure: several dozen NuGet packages used as SeroXen RAT delivery vectors over multiple weeks. Net targets included gaming, e-commerce, and cryptocurrency Windows apps.

entries.name: Microsoft.VisualStudio.Threading-confusion
entries.description: Dependency-confusion attempt against Azure DevOps internal feeds. Published a higher version to nuget.org than the internal package, hoping nuget.config priority would resolve in attacker's favor.

entries.name: System.Data.SqlClient-typosquat
entries.description: Phylum 2024 disclosure: typosquatted Sql client package with a credential-harvesting payload targeting .NET data-access developers.

entries.name: Bloomzeg-NuGet-Loader
entries.description: Reverse-engineering loader stub disguised as a build dependency. Downloaded an obfuscated stage-2 payload during `dotnet restore`.
